data.title: Citi is a prime candidate to revive big-bank M&A
data.description:
By Stephen Gandel

Though it denies any interest, the Wall Street giant would benefit from a major tie-up. Extra scale should help slash the high cost of its deposits, while regulators are as amenable as they’ve been since 2008. Combining with regional lender Truist could generate $40 bln in value.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Stephen Gandel on LinkedIn and X.

CONTEXT NEWS

Citigroup executives are considering whether they should buy a regional bank, Bloomberg reported on March 27. The bank's executives have reached out to regulators, who were receptive to the idea of an acquisition, according to the report.

Citi dismissed the report, saying in a statement to Reuters that “the suggestion that Citi is planning to buy a regional bank, wealth brokerage - or any other financial services firm - is baseless speculation.”

data.title: Why This Pharma Stock More Than Doubled Today — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Mackenzie Tatananni

Shares in small biopharmaceutical firm Apellis Pharmaceuticals more than doubled in Tuesday trading after Biogen said it would acquire the company for $5.6 billion. It's a big boost for a stock that had shed nearly a third of its value this year.

The commercial-stage biopharma company, which focuses on rare diseases, struck a deal to be acquired by Biogen on Tuesday. Under the terms of the arrangement, Biogen will pay $41 a share for all shares outstanding of Apellis — a steep premium to the stock's closing price of $17.09 on Monday.

Apellis stockholders will also receive a contingent value right for each share they own. This gives them the right to collect two extra payments of $2 a share each, provided that global sales of Syfovre, the company's geographic atrophy treatment, hit specific yearly targets.

It might seem like Biogen is paying a lot to acquire the smaller company, and Tuesday's stock moves suggest investors weren't too pleased. As Apellis surged 136% to $40.26, marking its largest daily percent gain on record, Biogen fell 3.3%. The benchmark S&P 500 was 2.5% higher.

Shares of Apellis have floundered lately. Heading into Tuesday, the stock had fallen 32% this year and nearly 25% over the past 12 months. Its protracted slump has only been compounded by recent risk-off sentiment amid the war in Iran, causing investors to flee speculative biotech stocks.

Apellis posted a 6% decline in total revenue for the fourth quarter. Syfovre sales alone dropped 8% from same period last year as a competing treatment from drugmaker Astellas continued to chip away at its market share.

Analysts have flagged concerns over the flat growth trajectory of Apellis' products. The company currently has two drugs on the market: Syfovre and Empaveli. The latter is approved to treat two rare kidney diseases.

Biogen stock, meanwhile, has gained 6.6% this year and 42% over the past 12 months, partly driven by better-than-expected earnings for the fourth quarter. The standout in its portfolio is Leqembi, an Alzheimer's drug co-developed with Eisai, which has shaped into Biogen's newest blockbuster medicine.

The S&P 500 has declined 7.3% this year and gained 13% over the past 12 months, meaning Biogen has outpaced the broader market over both periods. That's not to say the company isn't facing its own problems. Its aging multiple sclerosis franchise continues to suffer losses that must be offset by newer drugs.

If Biogen is looking for winners to add to its portfolio, the key could be Syfovre and Empaveli. Biogen expects the inclusion of both products to boost revenue immediately. The therapies brought in $689 million in combined net sales last year. Biogen added that it expects revenue is to grow at a mid- to high-teens percent through at least 2028.

The acquisition aligns with Biogen's focus on breathing life into its burgeoning growth portfolio, with a particular focus here on immunology and rare disease. Revenue for the latter sank 16% in the fourth quarter. The deal will also accelerate the company's expansion into nephrology, which began in late 2024 with the acquisition of Human Immunology Biosciences.

Write to Mackenzie Tatananni at mackenzie.tatananni@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Centaurus Announces Grant of Options
data.description:
Calgary, Alberta--(Newsfile Corp. - March 31, 2026) - Centaurus Energy Inc. (OTCID: CTARF) ("Centaurus" or the "Company"), announces the grant of 54,403 options to David Tawil, Chairman and CEO, pursuant to the Company's share option plan, exercisable at a price of USD$1.25 per share and expiring on March 30, 2031.

The grants were made in accordance with the terms and conditions of the Company's share option plan (the "Share Option Plan") that was approved by the shareholders of the Company on February 26, 2025. The Share Options Plan is a "rolling" 10% plan that provides for the grant of stock options ("Options"). The aggregate number of common shares reserved for issuance under the Share Option Plan, at any time, must not exceed 10% of the Company's total issued and outstanding common shares. For additional information regarding the Share Option Plan, please refer to the Company's management information circular dated January 24, 2025, which is accessible on SEDAR+ (www.sedarplus.ca) under the Company's issuer profile.

About Centaurus

Centaurus is a company focused on investing in physical and digital commodities. The Company's shares trade on the TSX-V under the symbol "CTA" and on the OTC ID Market under the symbol "CTARF".

FOR FURTHER INFORMATION, PLEASE CONTACT:

David Tawil, Chairman & Chief Executive Officer

email: davidtawil@ctaurus.com

data.title: Vycor Medical 10-K: Revenue $1.86M, EPS (0.01) — FY Summary
data.description:
Vycor Medical reported fiscal year revenue of $1.8634 million and a net loss per share of $(0.01), reflecting a modest operating profit but a small net loss after preferred dividends. Revenue benefited from an 18% increase in Vycor product sales while overall net loss available to common stockholders widened to $(396,796). Gross margins remained strong overall, supporting operating income improvement versus the prior year.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $1.8634 million (Vycor Medical product revenue up 18% to $1.79607 million; NovaVision $67,330).
  • Gross Profit: $1.561107 million; overall gross margin approximately 84% (Vycor ~83%; NovaVision ~93%).
  • Operating Income: $33,900 (improved from an operating loss of $9,145 in the prior year).
  • Net Loss (Net Income): $(72,426); net loss available to common stockholders $(396,796) after preferred dividends.
  • Net Income (Loss) Per Share: $(0.01) basic and diluted (weighted average shares outstanding 33,372,796).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Drivers: Vycor product revenue rose 18% year-over-year, driven primarily by increased international demand, particularly in Europe.
  • Geographic Mix: Majority of the sales increase came from international markets, shifting the company’s sales mix toward exports and increasing related logistics and validation activities.
  • Channel and Operational Changes: The sales lift was largely due to channel shifts toward international distributors and customers, which also contributed to higher related costs.
  • Brand Performance: NovaVision sales declined modestly (down 8%) to $67K but continued to produce a high gross margin (~93%).
  • Production and Cost Dynamics: Vycor experienced gross margin compression (83% vs. prior 89%) attributable to product validation, increased shipping costs and new production-related expenses.
  • R&D and SG&A Focus: R&D spending moderated for early-stage Vycor development while SG&A increased, driven by investor relations activity and stock-based compensation.

Original SEC Filing: VYCOR MEDICAL INC [ VYCO ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Investview 2025 10-K: Revenue $36.3M, Net Loss $8.9M
data.description:
Investview reported total revenue of $36.26 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, a 31% decline year-over-year driven by lower membership and mining revenues, and recorded a net loss of $8.91 million for the period. The filing does not disclose basic or diluted net income per share in the provided excerpts. Operational shifts and acquisitions during the year accompanied the weaker top-line performance.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue, net: $36.26 million, decreased $16.13 million (31%) vs 2024.
  • Gross Profit: $28.01 million (as stated in the summary).
  • Operating Income: Operating costs and expenses $44.516 million versus revenue $36.256 million (operating loss implied in summary).
  • Net Income: Net loss of $8.908 million for the year ended December 31, 2025.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not stated in the provided excerpts.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and decline: The revenue decline was driven by a 38% drop in membership revenue and a 36% decline in mining revenue, reflecting weaker demand and operational headwinds in those segments.
  • Channel and product pivot: The company redirected direct-to-consumer efforts toward health and wellness products and completed the acquisition of Renu Labs, which added a manufacturing channel and contributed approximately $3.6 million in product sales.
  • Acquisitions and platform expansion: Investview acquired Opencash, a broker-dealer platform, and completed the Renu Labs acquisition, including stock option incentives to management as part of the transaction structure.
  • Mining operations: Mining output was reduced by industry and regional factors — notably the Bitcoin halving, higher network difficulty, and regional power curtailments — despite higher BTC prices.
  • Regulatory and legal matters: The company settled an SEC inquiry, is appealing a PLN fine in Poland, and is pursuing litigation related to TPP affecting customer programs.

Original SEC Filing: Investview, Inc. [ INVU ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: First Guaranty Bancshares 2025 10‑K: $(56.0)M Net Loss, EPS $(4.17), NII $86.9M
data.description:
First Guaranty Bancshares reported a net loss of $56.0 million for the year ended 2025, driven by elevated credit loss provisions and a $12.9 million goodwill impairment; basic loss per share was $4.17. Net interest income totaled $86.9 million with a net interest margin of 2.28%, down from 2.47% in 2024. The results reflect a de-risking strategy that materially reduced loans while expanding liquid securities and altering the deposit mix.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Interest Income: $86.9 million; net interest margin 2.28% (2025) vs 2.47% (2024).
  • Net (Loss) Income: $(56.0) million for 2025, a decrease of $68.5 million versus 2024 (impacted by credit loss provisions and a $12.9 million goodwill impairment).
  • Net (Loss) Income Available to Common Shareholders: $(58.35) million; basic EPS $(4.17) for 2025 vs $0.81 in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Loan Portfolio Shift: Executed a de-risking strategy in 2025 that reduced loan balances by approximately 23% through payoffs, sales and targeted charge-offs, reflecting a deliberate reduction of risk exposure.
  • Deposit & Liquidity Mix: Deposits grew about 4.5% with a shift toward time and brokered deposits; the company increased holdings of cash and liquid securities to bolster liquidity.
  • Securities & Liquidity Build: Investment portfolio expanded significantly, notably with increased mortgage-backed securities to support liquidity and collateral requirements.
  • Asset Quality Actions: Increased workout activity including higher charge-offs and acquisitions of other real estate owned (OREO); management identified focused monitoring for commercial real estate, healthcare and hospitality exposures.
  • Branch/Market Move: Executed an agreement to sell Texas operations—five branches with roughly $270 million in deposits and about $110 million in loans—to streamline the franchise footprint.

Original SEC Filing: First Guaranty Bancshares, Inc. [ FGBI ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: BranchOut 2025 10-K: $13.7M Revenue, $(0.57) EPS
data.description:
BranchOut reported net revenue of $13.7 million for 2025, a 113% increase year-over-year driven by higher sales volumes and new product introductions, while net loss per share was $(0.57) on a weighted average of 10,734,295 shares. Despite improved net margin, the company recorded a net loss of $(6.1247) million and an operating loss of $(5.3635) million as operating expenses rose during scale-up. Gross profit was $2.0344 million with an adjusted gross profit of $3.8201 million reflecting margin improvements from increased internal production.

Financial Highlights

  • Net revenue: $13.7246 million, 113% increase vs 2024.
  • Gross profit: $2.0344 million; gross margin 14.8%.
  • Adjusted gross profit: $3.8201 million; adjusted gross margin 27.8%.
  • Operating loss: $(5.3635) million, increased from $(3.9024) million in 2024.
  • Net loss: $(6.1247) million, compared to $(4.7515) million in 2024; net margin (44.6)% (improved from (73.8)%).
  • Net loss per common share — basic and diluted: $(0.57); weighted average shares 10,734,295.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Higher sales volumes and launches of new BranchOut products and private-label offerings expanded revenue in 2025.
  • Channel and production shift: Transitioned manufacturing from third-party providers to in-house production at the Peru facility, with ramp-up beginning December 2024.
  • Manufacturing scale & efficiency: Peru facility scale-up increased the share of internal production, improving throughput and contributing to better gross margins.
  • Brand momentum and distribution: Expanded distribution with large national retail customers and broadened product assortment through new launches and private-label contracts.
  • Operational milestones: Completed build-out of the Peru facility and incurred start-up and idle capacity costs while optimizing production and logistics during scale-up.

Original SEC Filing: BranchOut Food Inc. [ BOF ] - 10-K - Mar. 31, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Ocean Re financial strength rating upgraded to A by AM Best
data.description:
By Chris Munro

Ocean International Reinsurance Company has had its financial strength rating upgraded to A from A-minus by AM Best, with the agency citing the Latin America-focused reinsurer’s “disciplined underwriting” and “strong” operating performance metrics for the change.

Barbados-domiciled Ocean Re offers a diversified product mix worldwide “with a relevant presence in Latin America,” AM Best said.

Ocean Re, which is led by CEO Guillermo Eslava, also writes facultative reinsurance programs alongside its treaty offerings.

As well as the FSR improvement, AM Best upgraded Ocean Re’s long-term issuer credit rating to a from a-minus.

In a statement, AM Best said the rating upgrades “recognize the disciplined underwriting by Ocean Re to deploy its capital with profitable results.”

“The company’s operating performance metrics are strong, in conjunction with growth targets based on its diversified geographical exposures, new business and successful growth of the risk-bearing portfolio,” it added.

Its risk-adjusted capitalization stands at the strongest level, as measured by Best’s Capital Adequacy Ratio, and the reinsurer has benefited from profitable results up to September 30, 2025, said AM Best.

At the same time, Ocean Re’s regional geographic footprint has continued to expand, with the reinsurer present in 86 countries as of December 2024, with the ability to further diversify throughout Latin America, Asia, the Middle East and North Africa, among other regions.

The upgrades also reflect Ocean Re’s “very strong” balance sheet strength, its neutral business profile and appropriate enterprise risk management.

data.title: With Prospective Plantings Out, Grain Traders Turn to Weather — Market Talk
data.description: Now that the USDA has published its projections for how much corn, soybeans, and wheat will be planted this spring, the focus among traders is turning toward the weather, says Brian Grete of Commstock Investments. "Weather will be a larger focus with the flip of the calendar to April," says Grete. "We'll start adding/subtracting acres based on weather, market prices, fertilizer prices/availability, etc." But Grete notes that with war headlines still dominating the news cycle, trader focus on fundamentals for grains may be limited. Late in trading, corn is up 0.2%, soybeans rise 1%, and wheat is up 1.2%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Today's Market: Dow Surges 1,000 Points, Nasdaq Jumps 3% as Trump Signals Imminent End to Iran War
data.description:
U.S. stocks climbed Tuesday as investors assessed prospects for an end to military hostilities with Iran.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose about 2%, while the S&P 500 jumped 2.5% and the Nasdaq Composite advanced nearly 3%.

The Wall Street Journal reported that President Donald Trump told aides he is willing to conclude the military campaign even if the Strait of Hormuz remains partially closed.

Technology stocks, which have faced pressure since the conflict began, moved higher. The Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) gained about 2%. Nvidia rose 1%, while Microsoft added about 2%.

Crude prices held onto gains after Bloomberg reported that Iran struck a Kuwaiti oil tanker in Dubai waters. Brent futures traded near $117 per barrel.

Consumer confidence edged higher in March, the Conference Board said, with its headline index reaching 91.8. Job openings fell to 6.88 million in February, according to Labor Department data.

The S&P 500 is on track for a monthly decline of about 8%, which would mark its worst performance since September 2022.

data.title: Upsales Technology AB (publ) delays publication of Annual Report for financial year 2025
data.description:
The publication of the Annual Report for the financial year 2025 is delayed from the previously communicated date of 1 April 2026 due to administrative reasons. The Annual Report is expected to be published on 21 April 2026.

As a consequence, the Annual General Meeting will also be postponed. The Annual General Meeting is expected to be held in mid-May 2026. Upsales will revert with a formal notice and updated date in due course.

Further information

All financial reports are available atwww.upsales.com

Contacts

ir@upsales.com

About Upsales

Upsales is a Stockholm-based software company on a mission to build the leading AI platform for B2B revenue growth. With best-in-class data and proprietary AI agents, Upsales helps companies accelerate profitable growth. In the last two decades, the company has built a track record of organic, profitable growth. Upsales is a net cash company with zero debt, strong cash flows, and has been paying annual dividends since 2022. Its scalable business model is powered by over 90% recurring subscription revenue. Upsales serves customers in 10 countries, with the majority of its customer base in Sweden. The company is 44% founder- and management-owned, with institutional shareholders including Danske Invest, Nordea Funds, TIN Fonder, SEB Funds, Herald Investment Management, and Schroders.

data.title: Jiayin Group ADRs Slide on Lower Profit, Revenue in 4Q
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

American depositary receipts of Jiayin Group fell after the Chinese financial technology company posted a lower profit and falling revenue in the fourth quarter.

Jiayin ADRs were down 10%, to $4.29, Tuesday afternoon. They have fallen 69% over the past year.

The company reported a profit of 100.6 million Chinese yuan (about $14.4 million), or CNY1.96 an American depositary share. That compares with a profit of CNY275.5 million, or CNY5.20 an ADS, a year earlier.

Revenue fell to CNY1.09 billion, from CNY1.4 billion a year prior.

For the current first quarter, the company is expecting revenue between CNY18.5 billion and CNY19.5 billion.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Swiss Market Ends On Firm Note; UBS Rises Sharply
data.description:
BRUSSELS (dpa-AFX) - Swiss stocks turned in a fine performance on Tuesday as signs of a de-escalation in Middle East conflict helped lift sentiment.

According to reports, U.S. President Donald Trump is looking to end the U.S. military campaign against Iran even if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed.

A report from the Wall Street Journal said Trump and his aides assessed that a mission to pry open the Strait of Hormuz would push the conflict beyond his timeline of four to six weeks.

The benchmark SMI, which stayed positive right through the day, ended the session with a gain of 108.12 points or 0.85% at 12,776.79, after hitting a high of 12,850.52.

UBS Group climbed nearly 4%. Senior Swiss lawmakers have reportedly assured the bank's executives that they would water down stringent new capital requirements. According to reports, the lawmakers have signaled a compromise on the lender's $22 billion capital plan.

VAT Group gained about 2.75% and Galderma Group closed nearly 2.5% up, while Kuehne + Nagel and Sandoz Group ended higher by about 2.2%.

ABB, Swiss Life Holding, Sonova, Helvetia Baloise Holding and Lindt & Spruengli gained 1.5%-1.7%. Lonza Group, Partners Group, Richemont, Straumann Holding, Novartis, Julius Baer, Swiss Re and Zurich Insurance Group also closed with solid gains.

Swisscom drifted down by about 0.8% and Givaudan closed 0.67% down. Geberit, SGS and Holcim ended modestly lower.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Snap stock surges 13% as activist pushes AI pivot, cost cuts
data.description:
Snap shares surged on Tuesday after an activist investor disclosed a stake in the company and outlined a series of changes aimed at improving performance and unlocking shareholder value.

The stock jumped 13.3% in midday trading, after Irenic Capital Management had built a position in the social media firm and was pushing for strategic and operational reforms.

Despite the rally, Snap’s shares remain down roughly 44% year-to-date and are still on track for a monthly decline.

Activist stake sparks sharp rally

Snap Inc. stock climbed after Bloomberg reported that Irenic Capital Management had taken a stake and sent a letter to CEO Evan Spiegel outlining proposals to boost the company’s valuation.

Irenic later confirmed it holds an economic interest of about 2.5% in Snap’s Class A shares and believes there is a path for the stock to reach around $26.37, significantly above current levels.

In its letter, the firm said Snap could be worth substantially more with changes to its operations and strategy. “Snap should be worth a lot more than $7 billion,” Irenic portfolio manager Adam Katz wrote, adding that its valuation could rise to around $35 billion if the company adopts its recommendations.

Investors reacted swiftly, sending the stock sharply higher as the market weighed the potential for activist-driven changes.

Cost cuts, AI focus and strategic overhaul proposed

Irenic’s proposals center on improving efficiency, optimizing Snap’s portfolio, and expanding its use of artificial intelligence.

The firm called on Snap to reduce costs through measures such as layoffs and to reassess certain projects.

It also suggested the company consider spinning off or shutting down its augmented-reality eyewear unit, Specs.

“Snap should not continue doing what it has been doing. It’s not working,” Katz said in the letter. “And we’re not telling you anything you don’t know already.”

The activist investor also highlighted the need for Snap to better capitalize on artificial intelligence opportunities, positioning AI as a key driver of future growth.

Snap has already taken steps in this direction, including a partnership with Perplexity AI to integrate a conversational search engine into its platform.

However, Irenic argues that further action is needed to unlock value.

The firm also encouraged the company to pursue additional share buybacks, building on its existing plan to repurchase up to $500 million of Class A shares.

Management response and broader challenges

In response, Snap signaled openness to shareholder input while emphasizing its ongoing efforts to improve performance.

“We’ve taken steps to improve performance, strengthen free cash flow, and offset dilution, and will continue to evaluate actions that drive long-term value for all stockholders,” said board chair Michael Lynton.

Snap continues to face structural challenges, including heavy reliance on digital advertising revenue.

The company competes with larger platforms such as Meta Platforms and TikTok, leaving it exposed to shifts in the advertising market.

At the same time, Snap is exploring diversification through initiatives such as augmented-reality hardware and new product offerings, though these efforts have yet to materially reshape its revenue mix.

While Tuesday’s rally reflects investor optimism around potential changes, the stock remains well below levels seen in 2022, highlighting the scale of the turnaround challenge ahead.

data.title: Nasdaq Leads Rally Built on Hope the Iran War May End Soon — WSJ
data.description:
By Rebecca Feng

Stocks jumped after President Trump told aides that he's willing to end the war without fully reopening the Strait of Hormuz, the narrow waterway that handled roughly a fifth of global energy flows before the conflict.

The Nasdaq composite led with a gain of more than 3%, with the latest bump coming after reports that Iran's president was open to ending the war; the Dow industrials advanced more than 2%, about 950 points.

Investors appeared to welcome Trump's stance as a sign that the current phase of the war would wind down soon. However, the trajectory of the conflict, and its implications for global markets, are far from certain.

Trump on Tuesday morning urged other countries to launch their own operation to wrest control of the strait from Iran. Overnight, a Kuwaiti oil tanker anchored off Dubai port was struck by a drone, highlighting escalating tensions in Gulf waters.

Oil prices fell: the most-active Brent crude futures dropped 2.6%. The price of the contract had recently risen more than 70% this quarter, on track to notch its biggest quarterly percentage gain since the Gulf War in 1990.

Meanwhile, U.S. gasoline prices crossed the $4-a-gallon threshold for the first time since August 2022, and the European Union's energy chief urged countries to conserve fuel amid a "potentially prolonged disruption" in global energy markets.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: DEQ: Profit jumped 74% on property revaluations and tax effects, with strong liquidity from a green bond
data.description:
Revenue and EBIT declined slightly year-over-year, but consolidated profit surged due to positive property revaluations and a one-off tax benefit. Liquidity and capital structure were strengthened by a €500 million green bond, and the company maintained high occupancy and a robust dividend.

data.title: Biogen's Apellis Deal Addresses Some Revenue Growth Concerns — Market Talk
data.description: Biogen's deal to acquire Apellis Pharmaceuticals for about $5.6 billion, should address some long-standing questions that investors have had regarding the company's ability to grow its top line, according to Mizuho in a note. Analyst Salim Syed says sales of the Apellis drugs Syfovre and Empaveli are expected to grow mid- to high-teens through at least 2028. The addition of both to Biogen's portfolio will immediately boost revenue, with the therapies having achieved combined net sales of $689 million in 2025, according to Biogen. Syed also says there's "obviously some debate on the price paid," for the deal. The $41-a-share cash payout represented a roughly 140% premium to Apellis' Monday close, as well as an approximate 35% premium to the 52-week high. Apellis jumps 136%, while Biogen is down 3.5%. (connor.hart@wsj.com)

data.title: 'One Can See Similarities to 1973,' Exxon Mobil Economist Says of Iran Oil Shock — WSJ
data.description:
By Jared Mitovich

The chief economist of Exxon Mobil, which is dependent on the Middle East for much of its oil-and-gas production, said the energy market shock from the ongoing war with Iran is structurally similar to the Arab oil embargo in 1973.

"When I look back on past recessions..., I think one can see similarities to 1973 in the sense that you're talking about physical barrels, physical cubic feet that aren't flowing," Tyler Goodspeed said during a discussion at the Economic Club of New York on Tuesday.

Goodspeed, who led the White House Council of Economic Advisers in President Trump's first administration, said that global output has become less energy and oil intensive since the 1970s, and many economies learned from the twin shocks of that decade by building strategic petroleum reserves.

Exxon Mobil gets roughly one-fifth of its oil-and-gas production from the Middle East, analysts estimate, and is poised to lose about $5 billion in revenue a year after Iran's missile strikes damaged Qatar's natural-gas facilities, The Wall Street Journal previously reported.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Cocoa surges, ends month with double-digit gains; raw sugar down
data.description:
Cocoa futures rose sharply on Tuesday, ending the month with strong gains, while raw sugar prices slipped from the previous session's five-month high.

COCOA

* London cocoa settled up 129 pounds, or 5.5%, to 2,487 pounds per metric ton. The market jumped 23% in March.

* Dealers said prices had moved to the upper end of this month's range after peaking at 2,494 pounds on March 17.

* The key focus in coming days is likely to be first-quarter grind data. European and North American reports are both scheduled to be issued on April 16.

* A decline of about 2% to 4% is expected in Europe while a steeper drop of 10% to 12% is anticipated in North America.

* Dealers estimate grinds to remain weak in the second quarter but expect a modest recovery later this year.

* New York cocoa gained 4.6% to $3,300 a ton. The spot contract rose 18% in the month.

SUGAR

* Raw sugar ​settled down 0.03 cent, or 0.2%, at 15.52 cents per lb, retreating from Monday's five-month high of 16.10 cents. Sugar gained 8.5% in March.

* Dealers said selling was emerging above 16 cents, which helped stall the run-up in prices.

* They said higher sugar production in key exporter Thailand had also helped keep prices in check.

* Czarnikow projected global sugar production to still be near a record in 2025/26 despite losses in India.

* The market has recently strengthened on rising energy prices linked to the conflict in the Middle East.

* White sugar lost 0.8% to $448.50 a ton.

COFFEE

* Robusta coffee settled up $74, or 2.2%, at $3,493 a ton, rebounding from Monday's 7-1/2-month low of $3,410.

* The market posted a 6% monthly loss.

* Dealers said fundamentals remained bearish, limiting the scope of any recovery in prices.

* Brazil is expecting a bumper crop this year, while the Iran conflict has hit demand in the Middle East.

* Stocks, however, remain at historically low levels in key consuming regions.

* Arabica coffee gained 2% to $2.9835 per lb. Arabica rose 4.8% in March.

data.title: Gas Just Hit $4 a Gallon. Is That Really as Bad as It Sounds? — WSJ
data.description:
By Justin Lahart

There is an argument that Americans shouldn't worry much about $4 gasoline. The people filling their tanks might not be convinced by it.

The average price for a gallon of regular gasoline hit $4.02 on Tuesday, according to AAA. That was the first time it has been above $4 since August 2022, when the world was still struggling to absorb the oil shock from the Russia-Ukraine war.

Until just a month ago, President Trump had won points with consumers for presiding over a drop in gasoline prices, which had fallen below $3 a gallon on average before the U.S. attacked Iran. The leap was swift and steep — and grating on consumers already weary from years of entrenched inflation.

In some respects, though, $4 gasoline shouldn't put much of a dent in consumer spending and the economy. When adjusted for inflation, consumers are paying less for gasoline now than they were in the past. Energy Department data show that regular gasoline touched $5 in June 2022, but adjusted for inflation, in today's dollars that figure was $5.56.

And even that is low in comparison with the inflation-adjusted $6.17 hit in June 2008, when surging global demand for crude, and stagnant supplies, were pushing prices higher.

Meanwhile, improved fuel efficiency means that people aren't pumping as much gasoline as before. In 2023 (the latest data available), the average light vehicle, new or old, on the road got 22.6 miles to the gallon, according to the Transportation Department. In 1990, when gasoline prices jumped in response to Iraq's invasion of Kuwait, helping to precipitate a recession, the average light vehicle got 18.8 miles to the gallon.

People also have more dollars to spend than in the past, so gasoline takes a smaller bite out of their pocketbooks. Last year, consumer spending on gasoline and other motor fuels amounted to 1.5% of personal income, according to the Commerce Department. In 2008, it was 2.8%.

But the run-up in prices has been significant — and fast. And dealing with a rapid gasoline-price increase is a lot harder than dealing with a gradual one. There is little that consumers can do to cut back on their gasoline spending in a meaningful way. People have to get to work. They have to pick up their children.

Weekly Energy Department data show that as of Monday, regular gasoline was averaging $3.99 a gallon. That was $1.05 higher than five weeks earlier.

Adjusting for inflation, that is the largest five-week increase since September 2005, when gasoline supplies were severely disrupted in the wake of Hurricane Katrina.

Gasoline prices can play an outsize role in consumer psychology.

Most people aren't making cold-eyed inflation adjustments when they are filling up. They just notice the price, and how high it has gotten. In March, Google searches on "gasoline" hit their highest level since April 2006.

Prices are displayed more prominently than anything else most people regularly buy. Gasoline is a frequent purchase, with many drivers filling up every week or two. Compare that with, say, a refrigerator, which many homeowners replace only after a decade or more, and so they rarely think about the price.

Economists have found that round-number prices for retail items have salience with consumers. A 2010 Brookings Institution paper found people were unhappier on days when gasoline rose above $3.50 and $4.00 a gallon.

Economic research has also shown that high gasoline prices can have ripple effects on purchases of other items. Car purchases tend to weaken, for example, perhaps because in their mental accounting, people place cars and gasoline into the same spending bucket.

Write to Justin Lahart at Justin.Lahart@wsj.com

data.title: Edison Innovations Renews Lumens Co. Ltd. for New 10-Year Agreement, Expanding the License to Include Additional GE Patents
data.description:
Lumens Becomes the First Global LED Manufacturer to Access Patents Originally Acquired by GE Licensing from Seoul Semiconductor Co. Ltd.

Now Part of Edison Innovations' Portfolio

DALLAS, TX AND YONGIN, SOUTH KOREA / ACCESS Newswire / March 31, 2026 / Dominion Harbor Enterprises' (DHE) (http://www.DominionHarbor.com) wholly-owned subsidiary Edison Innovations has finalized a renewal agreement with Lumens Co. Ltd., making Lumens the first global LED technology company to expand their license to the 20 original GE PFS patents to encompass the PFS (Potassium Fluorosilicate) patent portfolio acquired by GE Licensing from Seoul Semiconductor Co., Ltd. Dominion Harbor Group's Edison Innovations owns the patent portfolio following the company's 2025 acquisition of more than 2,500 patent assets that GE Licensing originally acquired from Dolby Laboratories, Inc.

The announcement of the license renewal was made jointly by David Pridham, CEO of Dominion Harbor Group, and Dae-han Yun, CEO of Lumens Co., Ltd.

"With this renewed agreement, Lumens is positioned to lead the future of LED technology with enhanced capabilities and innovative applications," said Pridham. "We are excited to empower Lumens with access to our expansive KSF patent portfolio, which is pivotal for the advancement of next-generation LED and miniLED applications."

This royalty-bearing renewal provides Lumens with unprecedented access to Edison Innovations' KSF portfolio, including rights to the patents acquired by GE Licensing from Seoul Semiconductor that are critical to the design and manufacture of next-generation KSF LED and miniLED applications.

"We are thrilled to have the opportunity to work with Edison Innovations and to access their groundbreaking patent portfolio," said Yun. "This agreement not only strengthens our position in the LED market but also enables us to push the boundaries of innovation and sustainability in display solutions."

About Dominion Harbor Enterprises, LLC:

Dominion Harbor Enterprises (DHE) is a premier intellectual property transaction and advisory firm, recognized for its unmatched expertise in IP transactions. DHE provides clients with unparalleled access to targeted and credentialed licensees, supported by its proprietary IPedia patent intelligence solution. The firm is committed to fostering innovation and growth in the intellectual property landscape.

About Lumens Co. Ltd.:

Lumens is a global LED company founded in 2004 with LED technology that has attracted attention as a next-generation light source. With the world's first successful mass production of LED BLU modules for LCD TVs, the company has grown explosively in the display field including such key categories as TVs, notebook PCs, and mobile devices, and has also expanded its business scope to encompass LED for lighting and automobiles.

US Patents licensed by Lumens Co. Ltd from Edison Innovations: 7497973; 11098246; 7648649; 10615316; 7358542; 9512357; 7847309; 10131835; 7453195; 9938457; 9455381; 9929319; 9184353; 10249801; 8981639; 10424697; 8829781; 8436369; 8362685; 8592232; 8237348; 8742449; 9680067; 8916898; 9698314; 9018669; 10230022; and 9741907.

data.title: Oil prices saw a record rise in March. Why the U.S. may not need to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description:
By Myra P. Saefong

Trump urges other nations to 'Go get your own oil!'

The Iran war fueled a record monthly rise in global oil prices.

Global oil prices in March cemented their largest monthly gain on record, following huge disruptions to energy producers in the Middle East and shipping in the Strait of Hormuz over the past month.

What began with U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran in late February has since spiraled into a historic global oil shock, resulting in the highest oil prices since 2022.

Now, President Donald Trump has told aides he's willing to end the U.S. military campaign against Iran even if the strait - one of the world's most important oil chokepoints - remains largely closed, the Wall Street Journal reported Monday, citing administration officials. By calling an end to the war, the hope is that it might keep the conflict within Trump's initial four- to six-week time frame, according to the report.

If the White House were to declare a unilateral ceasefire, and assuming Israel follows suit, Pavel Molchanov, Raymond James investment-strategy analyst, said he would expect Iran to "reciprocate" by halting attacks on its neighbors. That, at a minimum, would "avoid the worst-case scenario of widespread damage to energy infrastructure."

Under that scenario, Trump may not need to ensure the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz because Molchanov thinks Iran likely would once again start allowing the vast majority of commercial traffic to pass through it. Oil prices at more than $100 a barrel long term would hurt demand, and it "cannot be to Iran's benefit for there to be perpetual conflict with its neighbors" in regard to the strait, said Molchanov.

Tuesday's report that Trump may accept an end to the conflict without reopening the strait offered some tentative support to risk appetite. It's clear that oil above $100 a barrel is a "politically sensitive" level in Washington, said Fawad Razaqzada, market analyst at StoneX, in Monday commentary. That may explain Trump's "softer tone."

Global benchmark Brent crude's front-month May contract (BRNK26) traded at $118.89 Tuesday, up 5.4% on its expiration day. It was 55.6% higher for the month as of Monday, which would be its largest monthly percentage rise ever based on data going back to 1988, according to Dow Jones Market Data. Prices were poised to end Tuesday at their highest since July 2022.

U.S. benchmark West Texas Intermediate crude for May delivery (CL00) (CL.1) (CLK26) traded at $104.04 Tuesday. It's climbed 53.5% in March as of Monday, the biggest monthly rise since May 2020.

Hormuz ripple effects

In a post on Truth Social, Trump told countries that "can't get jet fuel" to buy oil from the U.S. and build up courage to take the waterway. "The hard part is done. Go get your own oil!" Trump wrote.

Read: Trump urges other nations to 'take' the Strait of Hormuz. Here's who has the most at stake.

Yet a full reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, which saw the passage of about 20% of the world's seaborne oil before the war, remains in question.

There's a possibility that oil prices could climb even higher if the strait doesn't reopen, Razaqzada said - and if oil stays high, inflation risks will start creeping back into focus and ultimately hurt risk appetite.

Data from Kpler show that 251 vessels, including those transporting oil and dry bulk goods, transited through the Strait of Hormuz from Feb. 28 through March 27. Before the war, the strait typically saw more than 100 vessels travel through in a single day, according to Kpler.

That transportation disruption, along with high energy costs, have swept up a broad range of commodities including natural gas (NG00), fertilizer, corn (C00), helium, sugar (SB00), and aluminum.

The energy story already has been rewriting the 2026 to 2027 supply-balance narrative for commodities, said Jake Hanley, chief growth officer and director of investments at Teucrium. "It appears most investors haven't caught on yet," he said.

Hanley thinks opportunities await commodity investors who understand the connections and can see the downstream impacts before they make headlines.

The global benchmark for liquified natural gas (TTFC00), for example, climbed around 59% in March, FactSet data show. Natural gas is a feedstock for fertilizer; fertilizer feeds crops, and crops feed livestock, noted Hanley. "The chain is long, but every link traces back to energy," he said.

Read: Americans are about to get a crash course in the global economy: Higher prices are coming for pineapples, plastic, chocolate and berries

The oil shock has been "massive" because roughly 6,000 products use crude in some fashion or another, said Edward Meir, an analyst at Marex. Transportation costs also impact commodities that are not dependent on crude, so it's no wonder that we have seen such a widespread impact, he added.

That means there are "few places insulated" from the impact of this oil shock, said Meir.

-Myra P. Saefong

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Luxembourg's CPI Property Group returns to profit in 2025 on positive property revaluations
data.description:
Overview

  • Luxembourg landlord's 2025 revenue fell 12%, reflecting property and hotel disposals

  • FY net income rebounded to profit, aided by positive revaluation gains and improved occupancy

  • Adjusted EBITDA and FFO declined, reflecting asset rotation and reinvestment

Outlook

  • CPI Property Group targets €500-€750 mln in disposals for 2026

  • Company expects disposal activity to accelerate in Q2 2026

  • Over €550 mln of disposals are under LOI or in advanced due diligence for 2026

Result Drivers

  • PROPERTY AND HOTEL DISPOSALS - Lower gross and net rental income was mainly due to disposals of property and hotel assets

  • OCCUPANCY GAINS - Occupancy improved to 93.3%, supported by higher office and retail occupancy

  • HOTEL PORTFOLIO SALES - Net hotel income fell 52% due to deconsolidation and sale of hotel assets
